goal: Add a runtime-dry-run executor mount lock that composes activation-lock evidence before any later runtime route activation, without executing runtime, mutating routes, calling providers, writing state, issuing permits, merging, or deploying.
scope_out: Do not execute runtime, mutate the runtime route, call providers, write DB/files/GitHub, issue permits, merge, deploy, change packages, change workflows, or alter retired sandbox write routes.
claims: Bluepilot can preflight runtime-dry-run executor mount readiness from Activation-Lock evidence.
The lock requires confirmation, operator execution ref, route gate ref, runtime mount ref, and activation_lock_ready for runtime_dry_run.
Even when executorMountReady is true, runtimeExecutionAllowed and routeMutationAllowed remain false.
assumptions: Actual runtime route activation remains a later explicit env-gated operation.
The existing /probe/runtime-dry-run route remains unchanged and default-off.
dod: GET /probe/runtime-dry-run-executor-mount-lock-contract returns the closed mount boundary.
POST /probe/runtime-dry-run-executor-mount-lock-preflight validates activation-lock evidence without execution.
Focused tests, typecheck, task-lock verification, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
required_commands: npx tsx --test tests/runtimeDryRunExecutorMountLock.test.ts tests/runtimeDryRunExecutorMountLockRoute.test.ts tests/runtimeDryRunRoute.test.ts tests/activationLockBoundary.test.ts tests/activationLockRoute.test.ts tests/meta.test.ts
stop_conditions: Runtime execution, route mutation, provider call, DB write, file write, GitHub write, permit issue, merge, deploy, package change, workflow change, or sandbox write reopening becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Operator Dashboard Activation Controls are merged and live. Runtime dry-run is the safest first executor class, but it still needs a separate mount lock before route activation.
